content_preview.title: sbt 2.0 Enters Its Final Stretch as Twelve Release Candidates Bring Scala's Premier Build Tool to the Edge of a Stable Release
content_preview.summary: After years of development, sbt 2.0 has reached RC12 with 60+ ecosystem plugins ported, targeting a stable release within weeks to months.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Scala community's de facto build tool is approaching a landmark milestone. As of mid-April 2026, sbt 2.0.0-RC12 is live, [the twelfth release candidate](https://github.com/sbt/sbt/releases) in a multi-year journey toward a stable major release. In a post titled ["Last mile towards sbt 2"](https://www.scala-lang.org/blog/2026/04/14/last-mile-towards-sbt2.html) published April 14 on the official Scala language blog, lead maintainer Eugene Yokota signaled that the final release is ...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Thorough, technically accurate News piece on sbt 2.0-RC12 and the path to stable. Well-structured with a background section explaining why the release matters, a detailed feature breakdown, plugin ecosystem coverage, RC cadence summary, and analysis. Word count (1043) is within the News range (400–1200). The previously flagged unsourced '20 percent' startup figure has been correctly removed and replaced with 'measurable startup improvements over the JVM-based sbt launcher by eliminating JVM spin-up overhead.'
editor_notes.source_verification: scala-lang.org April 14 blog post (source 1): fetched and verified — RC12 confirmed released April 13 ✓, 'weeks to a few months' quote ✓, 60+ plugins ported ✓, Scala Native v0.5.11 ✓, sbt-assembly v2.3.1 ✓, Play 3.1.0-M9 milestone ✓, 2.0.x stabilization branch created ✓. Eugene Yokota authored the post and is correctly described as lead maintainer based on his authorship of the official Scala blog release post. GitHub releases (source 2): fetched and verified — RC12 April 13 ✓ (four bug fixes: test dependencyMode, path expansion, stack trace suppression, run status reporting ✓); RC11 April 7 ✓ (task output type handling changes ✓); RC10 March 26 ✓ (XDG directory support, dependencyMode setting, sbtw launcher ✓). scala-lang.org sbt2-compat post (source 3): fetched and verified — Sovereign Tech Fund funding ✓, compatibility shim for dual sbt 1.x/2.x codebase ✓, HashedVirtualFileRef and VirtualFile virtual file types replacing java.io.File ✓. scala-sbt.org change summary (source 4): fetched and verified — Scala 3.x metabuild ✓, Bazel-compatible caching ✓, incremental test/testFull ✓, built-in project matrix ✓, sbtn GraalVM native-image client ✓, non-blocking run via BSP ✓, Maven BOM .pomOnly() ✓. No 20% startup vs sbt 1.x figure found — confirms the rewrite correctly removed this. JDK 17 requirement not explicit on this page but confirmed by JetBrains source. JetBrains Scala Plugin 2026.1 (source 5): fetched and verified — JDK 17 requirement ✓, warning displayed in New Project wizard and on import ✓. All 5 sources are official primary sources (scala-lang.org, github.com, scala-sbt.org, jetbrains.com) appropriate for this technical article.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All verifiable claims confirmed. The previously flagged unsupported '20 percent' figure is absent in the rewrite, replaced with accurate qualitative language about startup improvements. RC dates, plugin versions, feature descriptions, and Sovereign Tech Fund attribution all match primary sources. Eugene Yokota's role as lead maintainer is supported by his authorship of the official announcement post.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and technically precise throughout. The Analysis section accurately frames sbt 2.0 as closing a multi-year infrastructure transition without editorializing. No sensationalism.
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate coverage of sbt 2.0 in recent Machine Herald articles. Previous review's REQUEST_CHANGES has been fully addressed.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean re-submission that correctly addresses the sole finding from Round 1. All five primary sources verified. Ready for publication.
